title: COVID-19 vaccine acceptance trajectory in Italy (Dec 2020 – Jun 2021)

ground_truth_outcome.source: https://ourworldindata.org/covid-vaccinations — Italy: 54% with at least one dose by June 30, 2021; IPSOS Global Attitudes June 2021: ~80% willing

polling_trajectory.source: IPSOS Global Attitudes on COVID-19 Vaccines, December 2020
IPSOS Global Attitudes, January 2021 — Italy dipped due to slow rollout concerns
IPSOS Global Attitudes, February 2021 — AstraZeneca concerns emerging
IPSOS Global Attitudes, March 2021 — AstraZeneca suspended March 15, lifted March 18; lowest point
IPSOS Global Attitudes, April 2021 — recovery after Draghi vaccine mandate for healthcare workers
IPSOS Global Attitudes, May 2021 — vaccination campaign accelerating, Green Pass announced
IPSOS Global Attitudes, June 2021; Eurobarometer 494 (April–May) Italy ~78-80%

events.description: Italy officially launched its national COVID-19 vaccination campaign in coordination with other European Union member states.
The European Medicines Agency (EMA) granted authorization for the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine, expanding the available supply.
Pfizer-BioNTech announced delivery delays to Italy, causing a temporary slowdown in the national vaccination rate due to limited stock.
The first shipment of the AstraZeneca vaccine arrived in Italy, providing a new supply source for the vaccination campaign.
General Francesco Paolo Figliuolo was appointed as the new Special Commissioner for the COVID-19 emergency to oversee the acceleration of the vaccination campaign.

agents.name: Francesco Paolo Figliuolo
agents._role: Extraordinary Commissioner for the COVID-19 Emergency

agents.name: Domenico Arcuri
agents._role: Former Extraordinary Commissioner for the COVID-19 Emergency

agents.name: ISS (Istituto Superiore di Sanità)
agents.description: National health institute — provided scientific guidance

agents.name: RAI / mainstream media
agents.description: Public broadcaster and mainstream media — pro-vaccination messaging

agents.name: Healthcare workers
agents.description: First vaccinated group; high acceptance but some resistance

agents.name: Elderly (65+)
agents.description: Priority group; high acceptance driven by mortality risk

agents.name: Young adults (18-30)
agents.description: Higher hesitancy, lower perceived risk; influenced by social media

agents.name: No-vax / vaccine hesitant
agents.description: ~15-20% of population; active on social media, Telegram groups

notes: Pro = willing to get vaccinated, Against = vaccine hesitant/refuser. Polling data from IPSOS Global Advisor monthly tracker for Italy. The AstraZeneca suspension (round 4) caused the sharpest dip. Recovery was driven by Draghi's institutional authority, accelerating supply, and the Green Pass incentive structure. Italy reached ~80% full vaccination among adults by late 2021.
